title: Marge Simpson In Screaming Yellow Honker

jokes.text: I didn't think it was physically possible, but this both sucks and blows.
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on the terrible talent show performance
jokes.punchline: Clever play on words using opposite actions to mean the same negative thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that became culturally quotable. Perfect economy and structure with a genuinely clever observation about language.

jokes.text: Ha-ha!
jokes.punchline: Nelson provides his signature laugh as literal illustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration and economy. Nelson's laugh is his defining trait used cleverly in context.

jokes.text: Oh. Or should I say 'Grinskeeper'? No, I'm sorry. Groundskeeper was correct.
jokes.setup: Skinner attempts a pun on Willie's job title
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizes the pun doesn't work and abandons it
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner awkwardness - his rigid personality making him abort a failed pun attempt.

jokes.text: So, have you noticed... how North Edinburgh golfers putt like this... and South Edinburgh golfers putt like this?
jokes.setup: Willie doing stand-up about regional differences
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Willie demonstrating identical putting motions
jokes.explanation: Weak observational humor that doesn't land - the setup promises difference but delivers sameness without clever payoff.

jokes.text: Willie's not funny unless he's down on his knees... scooping up your little brats' puke!
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to angrily reveal his real job involves cleaning up vomit
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Willie's anger and class resentment breaking through his failed comedy attempt.

jokes.text: Kill me.
jokes.setup: Terrible talent show continues with more bad performances
jokes.punchline: Homer's flat, defeated plea for death
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and relatable audience response. Homer as audience surrogate expressing what viewers feel.

jokes.text: Who's on first? Yes. Not the pronoun, but rather a player... with the unlikely name of Who is on first.
jokes.setup: Chalmers starts the famous Abbott and Costello routine
jokes.punchline: Skinner completely misses the point and explains it literally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Skinner's literal-mindedness destroying classic comedy through over-explanation.

jokes.text: Well, that's just great, Seymour. We've been out here six seconds. You've already managed to blow the routine. Sexless freak.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' frustration escalates to personal insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character dynamics. Chalmers' exasperation with Skinner's incompetence is relatable.

jokes.text: You drive. I eat.
jokes.setup: Family leaving the terrible talent show
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple division of labor reducing life to basic functions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - maximum character in minimum words. His priorities distilled to essence.

jokes.text: Dad, you were supposed to pay for those snacks. I saw Krabappel's butt. I paid.
jokes.setup: Lisa calls out Homer for not paying
jokes.punchline: Homer considers seeing teacher's buttocks equivalent payment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic from Homer. The matter-of-fact delivery makes his warped reasoning funnier.

jokes.text: No. As soon as I get over, that lane will stop moving. Erma Bombeck said so, and Dave Barry agrees.
jokes.setup: Marge refusing to change lanes in traffic
jokes.punchline: Cites humor columnists as traffic authorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's relationship with pop culture and her driving anxiety.

jokes.text: Aw! You suck, Marge.
jokes.setup: Marge's hesitant driving frustrating other drivers
jokes.punchline: Direct insult from stranger
jokes.explanation: Simple road rage moment. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: So long, schlubs!
jokes.punchline: Driver's dismissive farewell to regular car drivers
jokes.explanation: Good setup for SUV worship theme. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Wow. Did you see that thing, Marge? So powerful. So commanding. So forceful and raw. It didn't ask. It just took.
jokes.punchline: Describes vehicle in romantic/sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's materialism and masculine insecurity through vehicle worship.

jokes.text: Oh, Canyonero. Go ahead. Drool all you want. You can't hurt that finish. Now, rainwater- that'll strip it right off.
jokes.setup: Gil bragging about the car's durability
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself revealing major flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil moment - his incompetence as a salesman undermining his own pitch immediately.

jokes.text: No! Wait! Ah, no! You can't take my sale! My wife's gonna leave me if I don't start bringing in the green. Come on. Let me have this one, Stan. I'm begging you. Look at me. I'm begging you, Stan.
jokes.setup: Gil about to make his first sale
jokes.punchline: Desperately pleads to keep the sale, revealing personal desperation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Gil's pathetic desperation. Character-driven humor that builds sympathy while being funny.

jokes.text: Whose voice is that? Is that Fred?
jokes.punchline: Doesn't recognize the voice of someone important in his life
jokes.explanation: Shows Gil's social awkwardness and failing relationships. Minor character moment.

jokes.text: Okay. You've got the undercoating... clear coating, rustproofing, under guard spray... Gravel Guard XJ, Vita-Seal, Repels-It... but if you want to protect the paint, you better keep it in this tent.
jokes.setup: Listing all the car protection options
jokes.punchline: Final protection method is keeping car in a tent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd car protection methods leading to impractical extreme.

jokes.text: Oh, then after your final monthly payment, there's the routine C.B.P.... or crippling balloon payment.
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully describes devastating financial trap
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about predatory lending practices. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh. Sorry, Homer. We thought you were one of those hot-to-trot soccer moms.
jokes.punchline: Realize it's Homer driving, not an attractive woman
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff about SUV demographic assumptions. Visual gag works well.

jokes.text: See? Instead of a cigarette lighter, it's got a lipstick holder.
jokes.punchline: Reveals obviously feminine design element
jokes.explanation: Straightforward gender stereotype joke. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Ah, crap! It's a girl's car! I can't drive this!
jokes.punchline: Complete rejection based on gender stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer masculine insecurity. In character but relies on gender stereotypes.

jokes.text: Oh, sure, you can, doll face. Pretty thing like you can do whatever she wants.
jokes.punchline: Treat him like a woman condescendingly
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the emasculation theme. Works well in sequence.

jokes.text: Are you saying I'm gay? Because if that's what you think, then just come right out and say it.
jokes.setup: Marge questions why he won't drive the car
jokes.punchline: Homer jumps to defensive conclusion about sexuality
jokes.explanation: Homer's masculine insecurity escalating to paranoia. In character but predictable.

jokes.text: Good Lord! How am I supposed to get in this beast?
jokes.punchline: Overwhelmed by its size
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Marge's transformation. Shows scale of the vehicle.

jokes.text: Well, that's a nice feature.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers the SUV has built-in steps
jokes.punchline: Understated appreciation for practical necessity
jokes.explanation: Marge's polite understatement about absurdly tall vehicle. Good character voice.

jokes.text: No one has ever asked me that before in my life.
jokes.setup: Car computer asks where she wants to go
jokes.punchline: Reveals no one has ever asked about her preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment revealing Marge's lack of agency in her own life. Dark but touching insight.

jokes.text: Ew! Why is Courtney Love on the Wheaties box?
jokes.punchline: Mistakes athlete for grunge musician
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that plays on visual similarity. Smart kid making cultural observation.

jokes.text: Hmm. I thought it was Sandy Duncan.
jokes.punchline: Marge adds her own incorrect guess
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing generational confusion about celebrities. Marge's dated reference is in character.

jokes.text: Do you want to spend your whole life doing what's right?
jokes.setup: Marge hesitates about cutting across field
jokes.punchline: Bart questions morality itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart line that's both funny and philosophically subversive. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Of course not.
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate agreement surprises
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline showing Marge's repressed rebelliousness. Great character development moment.

jokes.text: Out of my way, nature!
jokes.setup: Marge decides to drive across the field
jokes.punchline: Declares war on the environment
jokes.explanation: Fantastic character transformation moment. Marge's road rage beginning with absurd declaration against nature.

jokes.text: Look at me, Lise. I'm Baron Von Chicken Pants.
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous aristocratic title
jokes.explanation: Fun absurd title creation. Classic Bart silliness with good wordplay.

jokes.text: You're tomorrow night's dinner.
jokes.setup: Lisa says chicken is tomorrow's dinner
jokes.punchline: Bart reverses the insult
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comeback. Bart's quick thinking on display.

jokes.text: Mom! Bart took what I said and turned it into an insult!
jokes.punchline: Lisa analyzes the comedy technique
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary from Lisa. Shows her analytical nature while being self-aware about comedy.

jokes.text: A three-chambered peanut! Marge, look what I did! Come quick! Hurry!
jokes.punchline: Treats finding unusual peanut as major achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding wonder in mundane things and needing validation. Great character work.

jokes.text: So, lying on the couch and eating stuff isn't the same if you aren't around to see it.
jokes.setup: Homer wants Marge to come inside
jokes.punchline: Admits he needs audience for his laziness
jokes.explanation: Great insight into Homer's psychology. Shows his need for attention even in mundane activities.

jokes.text: Maggie smells bad, and the cat wants something, but I don't know what.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get Marge to come help
jokes.punchline: Describes household chaos with complete helplessness
jokes.explanation: Good Homer incompetence moment. His complete inability to handle basic parenting.

jokes.text: S.U.V. coming through. Open up for Marge. Thank you.
jokes.punchline: Politely announces her aggressive driving
jokes.explanation: Great character evolution showing Marge's politeness mixed with newfound aggression.

jokes.text: Geez. Try the gas pedal, Grandma. It's right there next to the brake.
jokes.setup: Driver frustrated with slow car ahead
jokes.punchline: Condescending instruction about basic driving
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage insult. Sets up Marge's response but nothing special.

jokes.text: Oh, for God's s- Go back to New Jersey.
jokes.punchline: Geographic insult as ultimate putdown
jokes.explanation: Basic geographic stereotype humor. Not particularly clever but establishes road rage context.

jokes.text: Come on, come on! Get that corpse off the road! The streets are for the living!
jokes.punchline: Declares segregation between living and dead drivers
jokes.explanation: Fantastic dark humor from Marge. Shows complete transformation from nice housewife to road rage monster.

jokes.text: Slow down, you maniac! Show some respect for this coffin full of bricks representing a young man lost at sea.
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate fake funeral setup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reveal. The specificity of the fake funeral makes it hilarious and unexpected.

jokes.text: Can the sweet talk, Thelma & Louise.
jokes.setup: Wiggum confronting Marge about road rage
jokes.punchline: References famous female outlaw movie
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Wiggum's character and the situation.

jokes.text: Why are the pretty ones always insane?
jokes.punchline: Wiggum calls Marge pretty despite her rage
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment. His obliviousness to the situation is endearing.

jokes.text: But over these next eight hours, you will be broken down to the level of infants... then rebuilt as functional members of society... then broken down again, then lunch... then, if there's time, rebuilt once more.
jokes.punchline: Describes absurd cycle of destruction and rebuilding with casual lunch break
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation. The casual mention of lunch in the middle makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Duh!
jokes.setup: Instructor explains obvious concept of road rage
jokes.punchline: Student's sarcastic acknowledgment of obviousness
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction to obvious information. Gets the job done but minimal effort.

jokes.text: Quiet, fatso! The sergeant's talking!
jokes.punchline: Marge aggressively defends authority figure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's aggressive side hasn't been cured. Unexpected rudeness from her.

jokes.text: Fire a weapon at your television screen... pick a fight with someone weaker than you... or write a threatening letter to a celebrity.
jokes.setup: Video explaining proper outlets for rage
jokes.punchline: Suggests increasingly inappropriate and illegal activities
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about unhealthy anger management. Escalating suggestions get progressively worse.

jokes.text: Um, Chief, can I at least shield my crotch?
jokes.setup: Eddie about to be beaten by students while in bear costume
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about protecting vulnerable area
jokes.explanation: Good setup for physical comedy. Eddie's practical concern is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Bears can't talk, Eddie.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum enforces absurd method acting rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic. Prioritizes costume authenticity over employee safety in most deadpan way possible.

jokes.text: And that herbal anger rinse just washed the rage right out of me.
jokes.setup: Marge reporting her progress to Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Describes anger treatment like hair product
jokes.explanation: Cute metaphor treating emotional issues like cosmetic problems. Fits Marge's character voice.

jokes.text: I won't even get sexual or nothin'- unless that's what you want.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately suggests sexual component
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's inappropriate behavior in character. Cringe humor that works for his character.

jokes.text: What would Curtis E. Bear do?
jokes.punchline: References ridiculous anger management mascot for guidance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to absurd traffic school mascot. Shows Marge trying to apply lessons.

jokes.text: We're free! We're free!
jokes.punchline: Prisoners celebrating freedom from minor traffic accident
jokes.explanation: Escalates Marge's rage consequences to absurd prison break level.

jokes.text: Ah, geez. Darned laminated- Would you mind? I got stubby fingers.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to tear up license dramatically
jokes.punchline: Can't manage it and asks for help
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy undermining Wiggum's authority. His incompetence extends to basic tasks.

jokes.text: Well, that's a turnoff.
jokes.setup: Marge says losing license feels like losing a limb
jokes.punchline: Homer's shallow response to wife's emotional pain
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfishness and lack of empathy on display. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Ah, what a gyp. They're all just lyin' around. Do something!
jokes.punchline: Expects wild animals to perform like entertainers
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his consumer mentality applied to nature. In character expectations.

jokes.text: I've seen plays that were more exciting than this. Honest to God. Plays!
jokes.punchline: Uses theater as ultimate example of boringness
jokes.explanation: Good Bart line showing his cultural biases. Theater as ultimate boredom is funny character touch.

jokes.text: But, Dad, lemurs are nocturnal.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to wake up sleeping lemur
jokes.punchline: Lisa provides scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa being the voice of reason. Sets up Homer ignoring scientific fact.

jokes.text: Don't worry, honey. Daddy will fix that broken animal.
jokes.punchline: Homer views natural behavior as mechanical problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic treating evolution as manufacturing defect. Perfect character misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I told 'em a chain link fence wouldn't hold rhinos. Oh, wait. No, I didn't. I meant to tell 'em.
jokes.punchline: Realizes he never actually warned anyone
jokes.explanation: Good structure with immediate self-correction. Shows workplace incompetence in crisis situation.

jokes.text: Jumanji!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to stop rampaging animals
jokes.punchline: Shouts magic word from movie
jokes.explanation: Homer applying movie logic to real situation. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Does anything from the movies actually work?
jokes.punchline: Lisa questions media influence on behavior
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary from Lisa about unrealistic expectations from entertainment.

jokes.text: That's your hat.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually Wiggum's police hat
jokes.explanation: Good visual misdirection gag. Simple but effective bait-and-switch.

jokes.text: She's good, Chief.
jokes.setup: Someone correctly identifies hat vs puppy
jokes.punchline: Lou's impressed by basic perception skills
jokes.explanation: Lou's deadpan follow-up works well. Shows the low standards of the police force.

jokes.text: Authorities say there's no immediate danger to anyone except those three luckless people whom we'll identify once the rhinos spit out their wallets.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of people being eaten, wallet identification method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - treats death casually while focusing on bureaucratic ID process.

jokes.text: When will Detroit build a sunroof for the husky gentleman?
jokes.setup: Homer can't fit through SUV sunroof
jokes.punchline: Calls for automotive accommodation for overweight people
jokes.explanation: Great Homer line combining self-awareness with consumer demands. 'Husky gentleman' is perfect euphemism.

jokes.text: It's not my rhino!
jokes.punchline: Disclaims ownership while being attacked
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities during crisis - avoiding legal responsibility while being chased.

jokes.text: Jesus, Allah, Buddha, I love you all!
jokes.setup: Homer trapped in porta-potty with rhino
jokes.punchline: Hedges religious bets in desperation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer panic moment showing his religious opportunism. Covers all bases in crisis.

jokes.text: One, two... three, 'B'!
jokes.punchline: Counting to the letter B instead of number three
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on counting vs lettering - good setup for desperate plan.

jokes.text: Well, I was watching Dateline... and Stone Phillips said S.U.V.'s always roll over when you turn sharply... and the gas tanks explode at the drop of a hat.
jokes.setup: Family asking how Marge knew her plan would work
jokes.punchline: Credits TV journalism for dangerous vehicle knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about sensationalist journalism. Marge's faith in TV news is believable.

jokes.text: And she also knew if a rhino sees a flame... he'll instinctively try to put it out. Stone Phillips again.
jokes.punchline: More absurd 'facts' from same TV source
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalating the absurd TV journalism theme. Lisa's matter-of-fact delivery works.

jokes.text: NBC has lots of great shows, and their news and sports coverage can't be beat.
jokes.punchline: Obvious promotional language in natural conversation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the network parody segment. Meta-commentary on product placement.

jokes.text: It has come to my attention that NBC sucks. I apologize for misleading you and urge you to watch as many Fox shows as possible. So, in summary, NBC bad, Fox good.
jokes.setup: Homer forced to read statement under gunpoint
jokes.punchline: Network competition joke with obvious coercion
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about network competition. Dark humor of forced corporate loyalty statement.
